intake.business_question: What distinct behavioral segments exist among active credit card customers, and what does each segment's usage pattern reveal about financial behavior, engagement, and product relationship?
intake.population: Active credit card holders with account activity in the last 6 months
hypotheses: High-balance, low-purchase revolvers exist — carry balances but rarely transact
High-frequency transactors exist — use card regularly and pay in full
Cash advance segment exists — primary usage is cash withdrawal, not purchases
Dormant or minimal-usage segment exists — low balance, low frequency, low payments
Installment purchasers form a distinct segment from one-off purchasers

decisions.detail: Excluded PURCHASES — collinear with ONEOFF_PURCHASES + INSTALLMENTS_PURCHASES (r=0.917).
Excluded BALANCE_FREQUENCY — 69.4% at max, low discriminating power.
Dollar variable outliers retained as plausible extreme values.
Iteration 2: k=5 rejected — no clean transactor segment.
Iteration 3: k=7 base with C0->C1 and C5->C4 merges approved.
All profiling findings accepted. Top differentiators: BALANCE_TO_LIMIT_RATIO (η²=0.717), PRC_FULL_PAYMENT (η²=0.695), BALANCE, CASH_ADVANCE, CASH_ADVANCE_FREQUENCY — all large effect, p<0.05.
S0 (High-Utilization Revolvers) and S4 (Cash Advance Revolvers) confirmed as risk segments — to be flagged separately in final report for Credit Risk team.

High-Utilization Revolvers.definition: k=7 C0+C1 merged. High utilization (0.76), low credit limit ($2,518), minimal purchases, PRC=0.01.

Low-Balance Occasional Users.definition: k=7 C2. Low balance ($442), 12% utilization, modest purchases, PRC=0.06.

Full-Payment Transactors.definition: k=7 C3. Near-zero balance ($103), PRC=0.77, installment-heavy, no cash advance.

Active Purchasers.definition: k=7 C4+C5 merged. High credit limit ($7,821), high purchases ($4,305), 58 transactions, PRC=0.20.

Cash Advance Revolvers.definition: k=7 C6. $5,165 avg cash advance, 47% frequency, $5,179 balance, PRC=0.04.

findings.approach_rationale: No target variable — pure unsupervised k-means on 13 features.

flags_resolved: micro-clusters C0 and C5 resolved by merging

key_differentiators.direction: Highest in S0 Revolvers (0.76) and S4 Cash Advance (0.61); lowest in S2 Transactors (0.04)
S2 Transactors dominant (0.77); S0 and S4 near zero (0.01, 0.04)
Highest in S4 ($5,179) and S0 ($1,823); lowest in S2 ($103)
Overwhelmingly concentrated in S4 ($5,165 avg); near-zero in S2 ($50)
S4 at 0.47; all other segments below 0.20
S3 Active Purchasers dominant (57.7); S0 Revolvers lowest (6.3)
S4 and S3 highest ($8,693/$7,821); S0 lowest ($2,518)
S3 highest; S0 and S4 lowest

segment_narratives.High-Utilization Revolvers: These customers are carrying near-maximum debt relative to their credit lines (76% utilization, $2,518 avg limit). They make very few purchases and almost never pay their balance in full (PRC=0.01). Moderate cash advance adds to their debt. Highest credit risk profile in the portfolio.
segment_narratives.Low-Balance Occasional Users: The portfolio's most passive segment. Low balance ($442), 12% utilization, modest purchase activity. Rarely pays in full but carries little debt. Highest risk of product abandonment if not re-engaged.
segment_narratives.Full-Payment Transactors: Financially disciplined. Near-zero balance ($103), pays in full 77% of cycles, installment-heavy, almost no cash advance. Generates no interest income — target for rewards and premium card programs.
segment_narratives.Active Purchasers: Highest-value customers. $7,821 avg credit limit, $4,305 total purchases, 58 transactions/period, $4,146 payments. Drives bulk of transaction volume and interchange revenue.
segment_narratives.Cash Advance Revolvers: Card used as short-term loan. $5,165 avg cash advance, 47% advance frequency, $5,179 balance, PRC=0.04. Clearest risk profile — warrants Credit Risk team attention.

hypothesis_results.H1_high_balance_low_purchase_revolvers: SUPPORTED — S0 confirms: $1,823 balance, $345 purchases, PRC=0.01
hypothesis_results.H2_high_frequency_transactors_pay_in_ful: SUPPORTED — S2 confirms: PRC=0.77, $103 balance. Note: S3 is high-frequency but PRC=0.20 — two distinct profiles
hypothesis_results.H3_cash_advance_segment: STRONGLY SUPPORTED — S4 clearest segment: $5,165 avg advance, 47% frequency
hypothesis_results.H4_dormant_minimal_usage: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED — S1 closest match; no fully dormant cluster (inactive pre-filtered)
hypothesis_results.H5_installment_vs_oneoff: SUPPORTED — S2 installment-heavy (0.63 freq), S3 one-off dominant ($2,839 vs $1,466)

flags_raised: S3 Active Purchasers: PRC=0.20 with high payments — short-term balance carry pattern, not chronic revolving. Confirm with Credit Card team.
S4 Cash Advance Revolvers: higher credit limits ($8,693) than S0 ($2,518) — limit assignment policy may not account for cash advance propensity. Flag for Credit Risk review.

driver_analysis.reason: No target variable — unsupervised analysis. Profiling serves the differentiator purpose.

recommendations.headline: Re-engage Low-Balance Occasional Users before they abandon the product
recommendations.motivating_finding: Largest segment (33.2%), lowest engagement, highest attrition risk

recommendations.headline: Protect and deepen Active Purchasers with retention and upgrade offers
recommendations.motivating_finding: Disproportionate interchange revenue; losing one customer from this segment costs more than any other

recommendations.headline: Convert Full-Payment Transactors to fee-generating premium products
recommendations.motivating_finding: High engagement, near-zero interest income — fee revenue is the only viable monetization lever

recommendations.headline: Refer Cash Advance Revolvers to Credit Risk for limit and policy review
recommendations.motivating_finding: High credit limits co-existing with extreme cash advance behavior and near-zero repayment

recommendations.headline: Monitor High-Utilization Revolvers for early delinquency signals
recommendations.motivating_finding: Near-maxed on low limits, almost no repayment — one income shock from delinquency

open_questions.question: Do Cash Advance Revolvers have higher income justifying their credit limits?
open_questions.data_needed: Income, credit bureau data, origination score, and limit assignment history

open_questions.question: Are Active Purchasers carrying short-term balance or beginning to revolve?
open_questions.data_needed: Longitudinal balance and payment data across multiple 6-month periods

open_questions.question: Is Low-Balance Occasional Users a stable segment or customers in decline?
open_questions.data_needed: Prior period behavioral data to classify trajectory — stable vs. declining engagement

open_questions.question: Flat silhouette curve (0.177–0.198) — soft cluster boundaries at S0/S4 and S1/S2
open_questions.data_needed: Boundary customers should be reviewed during campaign targeting to avoid misclassification
